ui.title: PROJECT INTERMESH
ui.download_log_title: Download your run log as JSON
ui.commit_subtitle: Lock this loadout and begin deployment.
ui.noscript: JavaScript is required to boot the Project Intermesh terminal.

prologue: Meshtastic is an open-source communication tool that lets small radios relay short messages from node to node.
It runs over LoRa (Long Range), a low-power radio protocol built for modest data rates across long distances.
Practical use: when internet or cell service fails, neighbors can still coordinate rides, food, medicine, and check-ins through a local mesh.
NOAA BULLETIN: DERECHO STORM FRONT PROJECTED TO IMPACT IN 72 HOURS.
Expected effects: grid collapse, cell congestion, road closures, multi-day outage.
You are one student with a soldering iron, a terminal window, and $400 of precious cash.
If the power dies, people will need a local mesh to coordinate rides, food, medicine, and shelter.
controls_lines: MOUSE    Click any option or button to select it.
[1-9]    Press a number key to pick that choice instantly.
↑  ↓     Arrow keys move focus up and down the list.
ENTER    Confirm the highlighted item or workbench loadout.
TAB      Jump focus between interactive elements.
intro.name_prompt: Enter your callsign to begin Project Intermesh.
post_name: Main objective: place enough Meshtastic devices with neighbors to keep people talking after the grid falls.
Failure risks: low coverage, weak signal, broken links, or an open channel that hostile listeners can exploit.

travel.free_ride: Yoshiko gives you a free ride to {title}. No travel fee charged.
travel.prompt: Travel planning for {title}: choose how to get there.
travel.insufficient_cash: You do not have enough cash for a ride. You head out on foot instead.
travel.ride_taken: You pay $10 for a ride and reach {title} quickly.

intro: ACT I // THE WORKBENCH
The online requisition terminal blinks to life. Every dollar spent here determines what the town gets later.
Use the workbench panel below to compare every purchase at once, then confirm when the cart fits your budget.
act1.budget_hint: Starting cash: ${budget}. Device selection and quantity are required. Everything else is optional -- tap twice to clear a selection.
act1.total_ready: Cart total: ${total} // Cash left after checkout: ${remaining}
act1.total_over: Cart total: ${total} // Cash left after checkout: -${delta}. Adjust selections.
act1.total_pending: Cart so far: ${total} // Pick a device and quantity to lock in budget.

workbench_rows.device_thinknode_desc: Walkie-Talkie style LoRa Meshtastic Transceiver with 1.54" screen, GPS functions, and case.
workbench_rows.device_techo_desc: All-in-one unit with screen, GPS and battery in injection-molded case. Features the low-power nRF52840 for long battery life.
workbench_rows.device_meshpocket_desc: Compact and portable device combining magnetic wireless charging with an integrated low-power Meshtastic device and a 2.13" display. Designed for everyday use, outdoor exploration, and reliable off-grid messaging.
workbench_rows.antennas_description: Ruggedized directional antennas that push your signal further through concrete, foliage, and urban interference. Each one can be deployed at a separate site.
workbench_rows.repeater_description: Expands the reach of Meshtastic networks with reliable, solar-powered devices designed for off-grid communication. Built for outdoor deployment, these devices offer durable enclosures, efficient power management, and flexible mounting options. Required for rooftop and tower installs.

locations.highgain_locked: Requires high-gain antennas add-on from shop

science.title: Campus Science Building
science.no_nodes_status: No devices left to give at this site.
science.no_nodes_line: No devices left to give. You cannot reach the science building.
intro: Destination: Campus Science Building.
Dr. Ansari leans in the observatory doorway, unimpressed by panic and very impressed by evidence.
"Give me one reason this belongs on my roof," he says.

choices.data_label: Appeal to telemetry and survival data
choices.data_description: Promise live weather relays, outage mapping, and a resilient data path for the whole town.
choices.data_meta_ready: Uses 1 unit // WisMesh Repeater installed // Roof install enabled
choices.data_meta_locked: Requires WisMesh Mini Solar Repeater for roof install
choices.emotion_description: Ask him to do it because people are scared and you need kindness right now.
choices.jargon_description: Launch into packet-routing terms and hope technical vocabulary carries the moment.
choices.highgain_label: Mount a high-gain antenna on the exterior wall
choices.highgain_description: Skip the roof argument entirely — mount on the courtyard wall with your directional antenna for a solid mid-elevation link.
choices.skip_description: Walk away from the town's best elevation point.

science.skip_line: You leave the science building dark. Full town coverage is no longer achievable.
science.deploy_roof_node_note: roof mount secured with professor approval (+{gain}% coverage)
science.deploy_roof_status: Roof access granted. The network now has a real spine.
science.deploy_roof_line: "Ansari nods once. "That is an actual argument." He unlocks the roof hatch for you.
science.highgain_node_note: exterior courtyard mount with directional antenna (+{gain}% coverage)
science.highgain_status: Courtyard antenna clears the roofline and opens a solid city link.
science.highgain_line: Dr. Ansari watches from the doorway as you bracket the antenna to the courtyard wall. He does not stop you.
science.deploy_fallback_status: Fallback placement in a dorm window limits the network horizon.
science.deploy_fallback_line: He refuses roof access. You settle for a dorm window and lose the best line-of-sight in town.

valley.title: Valley West
valley.detail: Residential area surrounded by signal-killing hills.
valley.no_nodes_status: No devices left to give at this site.
valley.no_nodes_line: No devices left to give. Valley West stays offline.
intro: Destination: Valley West.
Luz and Diego — your friends from anime club — meet you on their porch while hills crowd every approach to the neighborhood.
A standard device will struggle here. Luz already has the antenna bracket mounted and ready.
valley.prompt: Choose the Valley West deployment package.

choices.basic_description: Cheapest path, but the hills will likely carve out a dead zone.
choices.highgain_description: Stabilizes the valley link and pushes farther toward rural outskirts.
choices.solar_description: Best reach and long-tail resilience when the outage stretches past the first night.
choices.skip_description: Leave the valley district isolated behind terrain.

valley.skip_status: Valley West remains outside the mesh.
valley.skip_line: You keep your cash, but the valley edge falls off the map.
valley.basic_node_note: residential install, but the hills choke the signal (+{gain}% coverage)
valley.basic_status: Device placed, yet terrain still creates a valley dead zone.
valley.basic_line: The house line joins the mesh, and Luz hands you a warm mug from the kitchen. The western edge still drops packets into silence.
valley.highgain_node_note: high-gain residential relay clears the hills (+{gain}% coverage)
valley.highgain_status: High-gain antenna punches the valley back into town.
valley.highgain_line: Diego helps you sight the antenna line. The western district finally links cleanly to the main network.
valley.solar_node_note: solar repeater mounted on a valley rooftop (+{gain}% coverage)
valley.solar_status: Solar repeater gives the valley independent staying power.
valley.solar_line: The repeater drinks afternoon sun and throws packets across the low ground like a promise.

sugar.title: International Grocery
sugar.detail: Classmate with a hatchback and a full gas tank.
sugar.no_nodes_status: No devices left to give at this site.
sugar.no_nodes_line: No devices left to give. Yoshiko keeps her keys.
intro: Destination: International Grocery.
Yoshiko eyes your gear, then your empty wallet, then tosses you her keys.
"You give a device to the store, I drive you wherever else you need to go," she says.

choices.deploy_label: Give a device for logistics support
choices.deploy_description: Place a device at International Grocery, earn supplies, and let Yoshiko absorb later travel costs.
choices.highgain_label: Device + high-gain antenna for the rooftop
choices.highgain_description: Yoshiko climbs up with you and helps mount the directional antenna above the awning for a cleaner city-wide signal.
choices.skip_description: Save the device, but lose transport help and a busy community stop.

sugar.skip_status: No device placed at International Grocery. Yoshiko keeps her keys.
sugar.skip_line: You pass on International Grocery. The next deployments stay slower and more expensive in spirit, if not on paper.
sugar.deploy_node_note: International Grocery device online; Yoshiko now gives you free rides outside town (+{gain}% coverage)
sugar.deploy_status: International Grocery linked. Yoshiko starts driving your route.
sugar.deploy_line: Yoshiko tops off her tank and waves you in. The market goes live, and your out-of-town travel rides are now free.
sugar.highgain_node_note: rooftop directional antenna above the market awning (+{gain}% coverage)
sugar.highgain_status: International Grocery rooftop antenna broadcasts farther than the block.
sugar.highgain_line: Yoshiko climbs up beside you and holds the bracket steady. The antenna clears the awning and the signal finally reaches the next district.

apartments.title: Tesseract Apartments
apartments.detail: Community garden rooftop and student households.
apartments.no_nodes_status: No devices left to give at this site.
apartments.no_nodes_line: No devices left to give. The apartments remain unlinked.
intro: Destination: Tesseract Apartments.
Dalia meets you by the community garden with a crate of peppers, canned beans, and one stubborn smile.
apartments.prompt: Give a device for food and rooftop access?

choices.deploy_label: Give a device to the apartment community
choices.deploy_description: Extend the mesh to several families and bring home garden supplies.
choices.highgain_label: Device + high-gain antenna on the garden roof
choices.highgain_description: Dalia helps you mount the antenna at the highest corner of the rooftop garden for the best possible reach across the block.
choices.skip_description: Leave student housing without a relay and forfeit the food trade.

apartments.skip_line: You keep moving. Dalia watches you go with a basket that could have mattered later.
apartments.deploy_status: Apartment rooftop linked. Food stores rise with trust.
apartments.deploy_line: The garden device comes online. Dalia sends you off with extra supplies from the rooftop harvest.
apartments.highgain_node_note: rooftop directional antenna at the garden corner (+{gain}% coverage)
apartments.highgain_status: Garden rooftop antenna pushes the signal past the block.
apartments.highgain_line: Dalia holds the cable taut while you lock the mount. The rooftop clears the surrounding buildings and the mesh lights up another district.

radio.title: Radio Station
radio.detail: Strong reach if you are willing to climb the tower.
radio.no_nodes_status: No devices left to give at this site.
radio.no_nodes_line: No devices left to give. The station cannot be linked.
intro: Destination: Radio Station.
Geo unlocks a side gate and points toward the tower with a grin that says he would climb it himself if you asked.

choices.tower_description: High risk, high payoff. Best possible reach from the station.
choices.tower_meta_ready: Uses 1 unit // WisMesh Repeater installed // Tower install enabled
choices.tower_meta_locked: Requires WisMesh Mini Solar Repeater for tower install
choices.lobby_description: Safer, faster, and much worse for network geometry.
choices.highgain_label: Mount a high-gain antenna on the station rooftop
choices.highgain_description: No tower climb — Geo shows you a rooftop hatch and you bolt the directional antenna to the parapet wall for solid mid-height coverage.
choices.skip_description: Save money and let the radio station fend for itself.

radio.skip_status: Broadcast hub never linked into the mesh.
radio.skip_line: You leave the station behind. The town loses a strong mid-grid relay point.
radio.tower_node_note: tower-top mount with Geo spotting the climb (+{gain}% coverage)
radio.tower_status: Tower device mounted above the station roofline.
radio.tower_line: The tower sways, your hands shake, and the new relay paints a clean arc across the city center.
radio.highgain_node_note: rooftop parapet antenna — no tower climb required (+{gain}% coverage)
radio.highgain_status: Station rooftop antenna online. Solid reach without the climb.
radio.highgain_line: Geo unlocks the hatch and you bracket the antenna to the parapet. It is not the tower, but the signal is clean across the whole grid.
radio.lobby_node_note: interior station mount with reduced reach (+{gain}% coverage)
radio.lobby_status: Safe install completed, but the station never reaches full potential.
radio.lobby_line: You take the safe route. Geo does not judge you, but the coverage map absolutely does.

health.title: Women's Health Clinic
health.detail: Dense trees force a cleaner antenna setup.
health.no_nodes_status: No devices left to give at this site.
health.no_nodes_line: No devices left to give. The clinic stays outside the mesh.
intro: Destination: Women's Health Clinic.
Yasmin leads you behind the building where trees and wet branches turn the air into a green wall.
Dense foliage here demands better hardware than a naked stock antenna.

choices.basic_description: Cheaper, but leaves the clinic half-hidden behind the trees.
choices.highgain_description: Cuts through the foliage and gives the clinic a dependable route.
choices.skip_description: Save money and accept a medical blind spot during the outage.

health.skip_status: Health center left outside the mesh.
health.skip_line: You save the money. The clinic vanishes behind branches and static.
health.basic_node_note: clinic device placed, but the foliage still blocks clean traffic (+{gain}% coverage)
health.basic_status: Device placed, yet foliage still causes a medical dead zone.
health.basic_line: Yasmin thanks you anyway. The clinic joins the map, but every tree between you and town remains an enemy.
health.highgain_node_note: high-gain clinic relay clears the tree line (+{gain}% coverage)
health.highgain_status: Clinic relay pushes cleanly through the canopy.
health.highgain_line: The upgraded antenna slices through the foliage. Women's Health Clinic now has a reliable lifeline.

intro: ACT II // COMMUNITY DEPLOYMENT
Your city is on the map. Choose your route carefully: every stop costs money, shapes the mesh, and changes who survives the outage together.
act2.prompt: Select your next deployment target or lock the current network and move to diagnostics.
act2.finish_description: Stop deploying and find out whether the current mesh can survive the storm.
act2.finish_line: You close the deployment ledger and queue a town-wide ping test.

intro: ACT III // CRISIS AND TROUBLESHOOTING
You run a full network ping sweep as thunder stacks itself over the horizon.
diagnostics.issue_valley: Valley West packets fade into the surrounding hills.
diagnostics.issue_health: Women's Health Clinic traffic is vanishing into foliage.
diagnostics.clean_line: Ping sweep returns green across every deployed corridor. No dead zones detected.
storm_alert: Storm alert: derecho leading edge detected.
Grid instability spikes. Lights flicker across the city and then vanish sector by sector.
Project Intermesh becomes the only thing still awake.

intro: Mina from two streets over stops you on your way back.
Her family hasn't heard from her since the alerts started. She asks if you have a spare device — she wants to join the mesh and reach them.
mutual_aid.prompt: Give Mina one of your devices?
mutual_aid.grant_description: She becomes a node in the network. The mesh grows by one more person who can relay messages across the neighborhood.
mutual_aid.deny_description: You need every device for your planned sites. Mina nods and walks away.
mutual_aid.grant_line: Mina's face changes the moment the device lights up. She hands you a jar of honey from her kitchen. The mesh just got one person wider.
mutual_aid.deny_line: You keep moving. Mina watches you go. The mesh stays exactly as planned, and so does the silence on her street.

A.title: ENDING A // THE RESILIENT UTOPIA
body: Encrypted traffic hums through {nodes} devices placed with neighbors while the city reorganizes itself around mutual aid instead of panic.
The WisMesh repeater on the science building roof throws signal across every district in one clean arc — the highest point in town becomes the heartbeat of the whole network.
Neighbors use the channel to coordinate medicine, hot food, and shelter. By sunrise, the outage feels less like collapse and more like a town learning to move together.

B_fractured.title: ENDING B // THE FRACTURED LIFELINE
body: Your encrypted network works, but not for everyone who needed it.
The mesh needed a high-elevation anchor and enough devices in enough hands — without both, signal fades at the edges where people need it most.
The city survives in pockets. The people close to you make it through the night together, and the people just beyond your best signal do not hear the call.

D.title: ENDING D // THE DARK AGE
body: Coverage never spread far enough, supplies ran thin, and too many deployments were skipped or left incomplete.
The network goes silent sector by sector as the storm settles in and demand spikes past what you built.
Project Intermesh collapses before people can rely on it. Everyone waits in the dark for outside help that is still days away.

B_fallback.title: ENDING B // THE FRACTURED LIFELINE
body: The network helps, but only in fragments.
You proved the concept, but a missing high point, missing supplies, or too many skipped deployments leaves the city unevenly connected.
People remember your effort. They also remember where the signal stopped.

endings.final_stats: Final stats // Budget: ${budget} // Coverage: {coverage}% // Encryption: {encryption} // Supplies: {supplies}
endings.replay_description: Reset the terminal and try to secure the city with a different route.

intro: A rustle in the shadows. Something small is watching you.
A stray cat sits at the edge of the alley, tail twitching.
cat_encounter.pickup_description: Reach out slowly and hope for the best.
cat_encounter.scratches: The cat flattens its ears, swipes at your hand, and bolts into the dark. You have a small scratch and a lesson.
joins: The cat sniffs the air once, then walks straight to you and headbutts your shin.
